# MDPI Healthcare Submission Package

## Generated Files

- `V4_Healthcare_Submission_processed_MDPI.docx` - Main manuscript in MDPI Healthcare format
- `MDPI_Submission_Checklist.txt` - Pre-submission checklist
- `mdpi_reference.docx` - MDPI style reference document
- `figures/` - Directory for high-resolution figures
- `tables/` - Directory for table files
- `supplementary/` - Directory for supplementary materials

## MDPI Healthcare Guidelines Applied

- **Paper Format**: A4 size with MDPI margins
- **Font**: Times New Roman 12pt (recommended)
- **Spacing**: 1.5 line spacing for submission
- **Sections**: Numbered headings and subsections
- **References**: APA style formatting
- **Structure**: Table of contents with 3 levels

## Next Steps

1. Review the submission checklist
2. Add high-resolution figures to the figures/ directory
3. Verify all references are in APA format
4. Complete author information (if applicable)
5. Prepare cover letter for special issue submission

## Special Issue Information

**Title**: Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare: Opportunities and Challenges
**Journal**: Healthcare (MDPI)
**Submission Portal**: https://www.mdpi.com/journal/healthcare

Dear Duojie

Thank you very much for your new draft and sorry for the late reply.

I went through the draft quickly and wanted to share a few points:

The paper uses NLP and LLM to help older people overcome loneliness. Although some background information is given, it would be good to make the main research problem and gap even clearer, so that the reader immediately understands the novelty and motivation.

Similarly, the Related Work chapter could more strongly position our work within existing studies and highlight what makes our approach distinctive. 

I feel we should also give a bit more description about the PROMISE framework and the Zurich model. Of course, our method and workflow should be described clearly.

The results and discussion part could be made more narrative and reader-friendly, helping readers follow the key insights and understand their implications more easily. It would also help to connect the findings more explicitly to existing work and possible next steps. 

It would be generally great to add more figures and diagrams to show the workflow and results of our system.

Due to the limited time, I cannot review in much more detail at this stage. However, I believe this could be a paper that is suitable for MDPI. 

Let us try to submit to this edition (https://www.mdpi.com/journal/electronics/special_issues/S5P22A35UL) this year or early 2026. Could you already format the paper using the MDPI template after your improvement? Next round we should review and suggest all together to reach the goal.
It would be useful by looking at similar publications at MDPI to follow their paper structures. Hope this can be helpful at the moment.
Thank you very much! Look forward to hearing from you.
Best regards,
